# UC-17.1.41 · Threat-Centric NAC (TC-NAC) — STIX/TAXII Feed and Auto-Quarantine Effectiveness

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When a vulnerability scanner finds a sick computer, ISE should quarantine it. We check that the message gets through from the scanner to ISE — and ring the bell if the connection is broken or the quarantine never happens.*

---

## Description

Detects TC-NAC adapter connection failures, threat-list import failures, and gaps in the detection-to-quarantine pipeline (where threats are detected by a vendor feed but ISE failed to apply the corresponding quarantine action).

## Value

TC-NAC is the link between the vulnerability scanner / EDR fabric and ISE network enforcement. When an adapter fails or the feed import errors, ISE's authorization decisions revert to the non-threat-aware baseline — vulnerable endpoints continue to receive full network access. This UC catches the silent integration break.

## Implementation

Confirm the *Threat-Centric NAC* category is at INFO. Deploy the OpenAPI poller every 30 min. Schedule the search every 15 min, search window `-1h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression `vendor` for 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Disable a TC-NAC adapter (e.g. stop the Qualys API integration). ISE emits 83104 (adapter connection lost). UC-17.1.41 fires showing that vendor with adapter_drops > 0.

### Negative scenario

Re-enable the adapter and confirm ISE emits a new threat sync without errors. Alert clears.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- ISE TC-NAC license (Premier tier).
- At least one TC-NAC adapter configured (Qualys/Tenable/Rapid7/Cisco AMP).
- `tcnac_adapters.csv` lookup with `vendor`, `is_critical`.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Threat-Centric NAC* category at INFO. Deploy OpenAPI poller for `/api/v1/threatlist/source` and `/api/v1/threatlist/threat` every 30 min.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 15 min.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as in controlTest. Confirm alert.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — TC-NAC Pipeline Health":
- Per-vendor adapter status.
- Detection-to-quarantine ratio trend.
- Top-N endpoints by threat score.

Alerting: PagerDuty medium on adapter drop. Slack on detection-to-quarantine ratio drop.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **Adapter drops every hour** — vendor API rate limit. Tune polling interval.
- **Detection-to-quarantine ratio = 0** — Authorization policy not consuming TC-NAC threat score; review the auth policy conditions.
- **Feed-import failures** — vendor TLS cert change. Re-import vendor cert into ISE Trusted Certs.

## SPL

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (83100, 83101, 83102, 83103, 83104))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:tcnac")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event, count(eval(MessageCode=="83100")) as detections, count(eval(MessageCode=="83101")) as quarantines, count(eval(MessageCode=="83103")) as feed_failures, count(eval(MessageCode=="83104")) as adapter_drops by vendor
| eval coverage_ratio = round((quarantines / detections) * 100, 1)
| where feed_failures > 0 OR adapter_drops > 0 OR (detections > 5 AND coverage_ratio < 90)
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value: TC-NAC adapters healthy / total. (2) Sankey: vendor → threat_type → quarantine_state. (3) Top-N: endpoints with highest threat scores, last 7d. (4) Trend: detection-to-quarantine ratio over 30d.

## Known False Positives

- **Vendor scanner maintenance** — Qualys / Tenable / Rapid7 maintenance windows briefly drop adapter connection. Cross-reference vendor maintenance calendars.
- **Endpoint not currently on the network** — Threat detected by EDR for an endpoint that's offline. ISE has no session to quarantine. The detection-to-quarantine ratio drops legitimately. Maintain a `tcnac_endpoint_offline.csv` lookup updated from active-session data and exclude offline endpoints.
- **Newly added vendor feed before first sync** — Initial population delay; suppress for 4h after a new adapter is added.

## References

- [Cisco TC-NAC Adapter Catalog](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/products/security/identity-services-engine/index.html)
